module.name: prefer-object-spread
module.description: Enforce object spread syntax instead of Object.assign with an object literal as the first argument.

scenarios.name: should allow Object.assign with a non-empty first argument
scenarios.given: a call to Object.assign(target, source) where the first argument is not an empty object literal

scenarios.name: should allow Object.assign on a different object
scenarios.given: a call to MyUtils.assign({}, source) where the callee is not Object.assign

scenarios.name: should allow Object.assign with computed property access
scenarios.given: a call to Object["assign"]({}, source) using computed member access

scenarios.name: should report Object.assign with empty object and one source
scenarios.then: a preferObjectSpread violation is reported and autofixed to { ...source }

scenarios.name: should report Object.assign with empty object and multiple sources
scenarios.given: a call to Object.assign({}, foo, bar)
scenarios.then: a preferObjectSpread violation is reported and autofixed to { ...foo, ...bar }

scenarios.name: should report Object.assign with empty object and an object literal source
scenarios.then: a preferObjectSpread violation is reported and autofixed to { a: 1 } with properties inlined

scenarios.name: should report Object.assign with mixed object literal and variable sources
scenarios.given: a call to Object.assign({}, { x: 1 }, bar, { y: 2 })
scenarios.then: a preferObjectSpread violation is reported and autofixed to { x: 1, ...bar, y: 2 }

scenarios.name: should report Object.assign with only an empty object literal
scenarios.then: a preferObjectSpread violation is reported and autofixed to {}
